mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales signal growing consumer spending power, which drives corporate earnings and lifts equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: If RSAFS increases month-over-month but the S&P 500 20-day forward return is negative in more than 60% of such instances over any 3-year rolling window, reject this hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above the 5.0% threshold marks the transition from tight labor markets to slack, historically preceding recessions and predicting negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE crosses above 5.0 the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) across five occurrences, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deterioration in housing starts (supply of new homes) and rise in unemployment creates a negative feedback loop that depresses consumer wealth and spending, driving equity prices lower over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month decline > 2% and UNRATE month-over-month increase > 0.1pp
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger condition produces positive average 20-day forward returns in more than 40% of cases or if the interaction coefficient in a linear model is not statistically significant at the 5% level over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the inflation level is high (CPIAUCSL above 210 index level), aggressive federal funds rate cuts are interpreted as a panic response to a weakening economy rather than a proactive stimulus, leading to negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Reject if, under the regime of CPIAUCSL > 210, rate cuts of at least 25bp are followed by positive average 20-day returns (one-sided test p < 0.10) or if the signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals a contraction in manufacturing activity, which reduces corporate profits and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the trigger condition (INDPRO decline) occurs at least 10 times and the median 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not negative, or if the accuracy of negative predictions is below 55%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising payroll employment and collapsing housing starts reflects an unsustainable late-cycle dynamic; when both occur simultaneously, it signals an impending downturn that manifests in negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not lead to negative 60-day returns in at least 70% of instances (minimum 5 instances) or if the correlation between the divergence measure and forward returns is insignificant (|r| < 0.2).

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth sustains corporate earnings and investor confidence, supporting higher equity prices over the subsequent quarter as the economic expansion continues.
failure_condition: If the direction of GDPC1 change (positive vs negative) has no statistically significant predictive accuracy for 60-day forward S&P 500 returns above 55% over a 10-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: An aggressive monthly federal funds rate cut of at least 50 basis points signals that the Federal Reserve is reacting to acute financial distress, which triggers short-term risk aversion and negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: If after a 50bp+ cut the average 5-day forward S&P 500 return is positive or not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) over at least 3 such events, reject.
